note: A generator inlines the compact_prompt fragments of the selected patterns into its prompt. It MUST NOT depend on fetching this file at run time; links are optional enrichment. Patterns with converter_ready=false are held back from prompt packs until the converter supports them.

support_legend.partial: converts, but a dedicated pattern/converter pass would improve it
support_legend.gap: needs converter work before it converts well

families.label: Consulting / strategy
families.family_rule: Action titles: each slide title is a full-sentence finding, and the titles read top-to-bottom as the whole argument. One message per slide. Answer first (pyramid), then MECE support, then next steps. Evidence sits beside its claim; every data slide carries a 'Source:' line. Colour directs attention, it does not decorate.

archetypes.name: Boardroom Classic
archetypes.best_for: recommendations, value cases, market sizing, M&A logic
archetypes.visual_language: white, restrained, dense evidence, footnotes, blue/grey accent

archetypes.name: Modern Insight
archetypes.best_for: research readouts, thought leadership, executive insight
archetypes.visual_language: open whitespace, simple charts, one confident accent, few boxes, small multiples

archetypes.name: Operator Redline
archetypes.best_for: commercial strategy, market studies, pragmatic recommendations
archetypes.visual_language: strong top rule, red accent used sparingly, direct ranked charts, source/confidentiality footer

archetypes.name: Research Bureau
archetypes.best_for: public research, survey decks, institute reports
archetypes.visual_language: chapter navigation, full-bleed section dividers with a large numeral, methodology, report flow

archetypes.name: Systems Blueprint
archetypes.visual_language: modular boxes, swimlanes, capability maps, more tables and diagrams, strong section labels

archetypes.name: Executive Brief
archetypes.best_for: leadership updates, macro briefings, board memos
archetypes.visual_language: blue/neutral accent, dense-but-readable memo slides, executive summaries, section dividers, strict hierarchy

patterns.name: Executive summary
patterns.compact_prompt: Executive summary: open with the recommendation in one bold line, then 3-5 supporting findings as a numbered list or a stat row. State the answer first; the rest of the deck defends it.

patterns.name: Action-title evidence slide
patterns.job: Put one claim and its proof together
patterns.compact_prompt: Action-title evidence slide: the title states the finding as a full sentence; use data-unpaper-lay='2l' with a short so-what (2-3 bullets) on the left and ONE proof object (chart or table) on the right; put a 'Source:' line directly under the evidence.

patterns.name: Single chart proof
patterns.compact_prompt: Single chart proof: one data-unpaper-chart carries the whole point; the title states what the chart proves; one source line below; no second visual.

patterns.name: Small multiples
patterns.job: Compare several charts on one scale
patterns.compact_prompt: Small multiples: 2-4 charts of the SAME type and identical scale side by side (data-unpaper-lay='2' or '3') to compare segments or periods; one shared source line.

patterns.name: Waterfall / bridge
patterns.job: Explain change from baseline to outcome
patterns.compact_prompt: Waterfall/bridge: show how a total moves from a start value to an end value through +/- steps, with running-total bars and connectors; label each step's delta and the start/end totals.

patterns.name: Market map / 2x2
patterns.compact_prompt: 2x2 matrix: position options on two named axes using the matrix block (.matrix.axes) with four quadrants, clear axis labels, and the priority cell marked; plot 4-8 items max.

patterns.name: Issue tree
patterns.job: Break a problem into MECE branches
patterns.compact_prompt: Issue tree: break the question into MECE branches (a parent box over 2-4 child boxes, each splitting further if needed) using the org/tree block; branches must be mutually exclusive and collectively exhaustive.

patterns.name: Option comparison
patterns.compact_prompt: Option comparison: a real <table> with options as rows and criteria as columns (<=6 columns); mark the recommended row or column; one source line below.

patterns.name: Scorecard / Harvey balls
patterns.compact_prompt: Scorecard: a real <table> of options x criteria; each cell a Harvey-ball rating (data-unpaper-rating 0-1, drawn as a filled-pie SVG: empty, quarter, half, three-quarter, full) or a RAG colour; add a one-line legend and a source line.

patterns.name: Maturity ladder
patterns.job: Show levels from current to target state

patterns.name: Roadmap / phases
patterns.compact_prompt: Roadmap: 3-5 phases over time using the timeline block, each with a date/label and 1-2 outcomes, laid out left to right.

patterns.name: Process flow
patterns.compact_prompt: Process flow: 3-5 ordered steps joined by arrows using the flow block; one short label per step; arrows in the accent colour.

patterns.name: Initiative portfolio
patterns.job: Sort initiatives by impact, effort, status
patterns.compact_prompt: Initiative portfolio: sort ~6-8 initiatives by impact x effort on a 2x2, or as status-tagged cards; mark the quick wins.

patterns.name: Business case / value sizing
patterns.job: Explain value pool and financial impact

patterns.name: Risk and mitigation
patterns.job: Pair risks with controls and actions

patterns.name: Case example
patterns.job: Show one proof point or vignette

patterns.name: Recommendation / decision
patterns.job: Close with the ask, decision, next step
patterns.compact_prompt: Recommendation close: the decision/ask in one bold line, the 2-3 reasons, and the first concrete next step with an owner and a date.
